2606.09605.title: Next-Token Prediction Learns Generalisable Representations of Sleep Physiology
2606.09605.paper_summary: Foundation models offer a promising route to compress multi-modal physiological signals into compact representations of human health, with broad applications across sleep medicine, cardiology, neurology and other healthcare domains. Existing models have typically been trained with masked-reconstruction or contrastive objectives. However, masked reconstruction may be poorly suited to the stochastic nature of these signals, while contrastive approaches rely on positive-pair definitions despite the semantic invariances of physiological signals being poorly understood. In this work, we show that next-token prediction is a simple and scalable alternative. We develop Hypnos, a multi-modal sleep foundation model trained using eight different sensing modalities (e.g. EEG, ECG, respiratory signals) drawn from over 20,000 overnight polysomnography recordings. We tokenize each modality into streams of discrete tokens using residual vector quantization, then train a large auto-regressive RQ-Transformer to jointly predict the next token across all modalities in parallel. After training, Hypnos can be applied to continuous streams of sensor data from any subset of supported modalities, generating embeddings for downstream tasks. Across a range of benchmarks, Hypnos significantly outperforms existing foundation models. In sleep stage classification, we match the performance of strong supervised baselines on held-out test sets whilst using \(100\times\) less labelled data. Hypnos even generalises to daytime physiology, surpassing a dedicated ECG foundation model at detecting atrial fibrillation. Our results demonstrate that next-token prediction is a strong self-supervised objective for representation learning from multi-modal physiological signals.
2606.09605.paper_summary_zh: 基礎模型提供了一條有前景的途徑，將多模態生理信號壓縮為人類健康的緊湊表示，並在睡眠醫學、心臟病學、神經學及其他醫療領域中具有廣泛的應用。現有模型通常是通過遮蔽重建或對比目標進行訓練。然而，遮蔽重建可能不太適合這些信號的隨機性質，而對比方法則依賴於正對的定義，儘管生理信號的語義不變性尚未被很好理解。在這項工作中，我們展示了下一個標記預測是一種簡單且可擴展的替代方案。我們開發了Hypnos，一個多模態睡眠基礎模型，使用來自超過20,000個過夜多導睡眠檢查記錄的八種不同感測模態（例如EEG、ECG、呼吸信號）進行訓練。我們將每種模態標記為離散標記的流，使用殘差向量量化，然後訓練一個大型自回歸RQ-Transformer，以並行方式共同預測所有模態中的下一個標記。訓練後，Hypnos可以應用於任何支持模態的連續傳感器數據流，為下游任務生成嵌入。在一系列基準測試中，Hypnos顯著超越了現有的基礎模型。在睡眠階段分類中，我們在保留的測試集上達到了強大的監督基準的性能，同時使用的標記數據少了\(100\times\)。Hypnos甚至能夠推廣到白天生理學，超越了一個專門的ECG基礎模型在檢測心房顫動方面的表現。我們的結果表明，下一個標記預測是一個強大的自我監督目標，用於從多模態生理信號中進行表示學習。
2606.09605.author: Jonathan F. Carter et.al.
